data.title: New Zealand Rural Land Co. Releases 2025 Climate-Related Disclosures Report
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

New Zealand Rural Land Co. Ltd. ( (NZ:NZL) ) has shared an update.

New Zealand Rural Land Company Limited has released its 2025 Climate-Related Disclosures Report, outlining how climate change factors into its governance, strategy, risk management, and metrics and targets. The report frames climate as a financially material issue for the long-term value and resilience of its rural land portfolio, and details how diversification across land uses and regions, tenant engagement, and initiatives such as forestry expansion and native regeneration support climate resilience and value preservation.

The disclosure includes climate scenario analysis, identification of climate-related risks and opportunities, and a greenhouse gas inventory for the year ended 31 December 2025. It highlights priority decarbonisation and resilience activities, including land-use positioning, carbon removals, and adaptation to physical climate risks, signalling an ongoing integration of climate considerations into capital allocation and portfolio development decisions.

More about New Zealand Rural Land Co. Ltd.

New Zealand Rural Land Company Limited is a rural land investment business focused on building and managing a diversified portfolio of high-quality agricultural and forestry assets across New Zealand. The company owns more than 17,000 hectares of rural land, which it leases under long-term arrangements, positioning itself as a landowner rather than an operator and influencing outcomes through land selection, land-use mix, and tenant relationships.

Average Trading Volume: 144,647

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold

Current Market Cap: N$134.4M

data.title: SIV Capital Posts Modest Cash Outflow Amid Quiet Investment and Funding Quarter
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

SIV Capital Limited ( (AU:SIV) ) has shared an update.

SIV Capital Limited has reported modest operating cash inflows from customers and interest income for the quarter ended 31 March 2026, but these were outweighed by legal and other administrative costs. As a result, the company recorded a net operating cash outflow of A$86,000 for the quarter and A$311,000 year-to-date, while registering no cash movements from investing or financing activities, underscoring a period of limited capital deployment and a continued emphasis on managing overheads.

The cash flow profile indicates that SIV Capital is currently sustaining its operations primarily through existing cash resources and interest income rather than new investments or borrowings. This pattern may signal a cautious financial stance or a transitional phase in which the company is focused on controlling expenses, with stakeholders likely to monitor how prolonged outflows and the absence of growth-oriented spending could affect future liquidity and strategic flexibility.

More about SIV Capital Limited

SIV Capital Limited is an ASX-listed company, but the provided filing offers no description of its industry, principal activities, or core products and services. As the document is a standard Appendix 4C quarterly cash flow report, it focuses solely on financial metrics rather than operational or market details, leaving the company’s specific market focus unidentified.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Sell

Current Market Cap: A$7.1M

data.title: Stavely Minerals Posts Continued Cash Outflows as Exploration Funding Relies on External Capital
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Stavely Minerals Ltd ( (AU:SVY) ) just unveiled an update.

Stavely Minerals reported a quarterly cash outflow from operating activities of A$438,000 for the March 2026 quarter, driven mainly by exploration, exploration staff, and corporate expenses, against modest receipts and interest income. The company recorded minimal investing activity aside from a small cash bond release, while financing inflows, including prior equity raising and borrowings, helped limit the net decrease in cash, leaving A$471,000 in cash and equivalents at period end.

Over the nine months to March 2026, cumulative operating cash outflows reached A$2.915 million, partially offset by A$1.955 million of net financing inflows, underscoring Stavely Minerals’ reliance on external capital to fund its exploration program. The cash burn profile highlights the ongoing funding needs and typical risk-return dynamics faced by exploration-focused miners, with liquidity management remaining a key consideration for shareholders and potential investors.

More about Stavely Minerals Ltd

Stavely Minerals Limited is an Australian mining exploration company focused on mineral discovery and evaluation activities. The company generates limited operating receipts and primarily deploys capital into exploration, staff, and corporate costs typical of early-stage resource explorers rather than revenue-generating production operations.

data.title: ACC stake in KMD Brands diluted after retail entitlement offer
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from KMD Brands Limited ( (NZ:KMD) ) is now available.

KMD Brands Limited, a dual-listed outdoor and lifestyle apparel group, operates primarily through branded retail and wholesale channels across New Zealand, Australia and other markets. Its core business centres on designing and selling clothing and equipment aimed at travel, adventure and outdoor-focused consumers.

Accident Compensation Corporation has disclosed that its substantial holding in KMD Brands has been diluted from 11.628% to 9.355% following the issue of 351,832,889 new shares under the retail component of KMD’s underwritten accelerated renounceable entitlement offer. ACC did not participate in the retail tranche, having only taken part in the institutional component, so its absolute shareholding is unchanged but now represents a smaller proportion of KMD’s expanded capital base, modestly reshaping the company’s ownership structure for existing shareholders.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:KMD) stock is a Buy with a N$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KMD Brands Limited stock, see the NZ:KMD Stock Forecast page.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited is a listed retailer and brand owner in the outdoor and lifestyle apparel sector, with its ordinary shares quoted on both the NZX and ASX. The company targets consumers in New Zealand, Australia and other markets through its portfolio of brands, focusing on clothing and equipment for travel, adventure and everyday outdoor use.

data.title: Syntara to Brief Investors on FDA Feedback and Capital Raising in Upcoming Webinar
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Syntara Limited ( (AU:SNT) ) has issued an update.

Syntara Limited will host an investor webinar on 30 April 2026 to brief shareholders and the market on recent developments, including positive U.S. FDA feedback on its planned Phase 2b trial of lead candidate amsulostat in myelofibrosis and details of a recent capital raising. CEO Gary Phillips will lead the session, and a replay will be made available online, signaling the company’s effort to deepen engagement with investors as it advances its clinical pipeline.

The update comes as Syntara progresses amsulostat through mid-stage development in myelofibrosis and myelodysplastic syndrome, while also pushing forward early-stage programs in scarring, neurodegeneration and systemic fibrotic diseases. By highlighting regulatory milestones and funding initiatives, the webinar is likely to give investors greater visibility on Syntara’s development trajectory and its strategic positioning in oncology, fibrosis and neuroinflammation drug development.

The most recent analyst rating on (AU:SNT) stock is a Buy with a A$0.19 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Syntara Limited stock, see the AU:SNT Stock Forecast page.

More about Syntara Limited

Syntara Limited is an ASX-listed clinical-stage drug development company focused on targeting extracellular matrix dysfunction using its expertise in amine oxidase chemistry and related technologies. Its pipeline targets blood cancers and diseases linked to inflammation and fibrosis, with lead candidate amsulostat in myelofibrosis and additional programs in myelodysplastic syndrome, scarring, neuroinflammation and fibrotic conditions, supported by a Sydney-based management and scientific team.

The company is also advancing topical pan-LOX inhibitors for hypertrophic and keloid scars, and SNT-4728 for sleep disorders and neurodegenerative diseases such as Parkinson’s via a partnership with Parkinson’s UK. Beyond blood cancers, Syntara is developing candidates for kidney, liver, pulmonary and cardiac fibrosis, having previously developed and sold respiratory products Bronchitol and Aridol, which are now available in global markets.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: A$52.3M

data.title: Institutional investors disclose reshaped stakes in KMD Brands
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

KMD Brands Limited ( (NZ:KMD) ) has issued an update.

FirstCape Group Limited and Harbour Asset Management Limited have disclosed changes in their substantial shareholdings in KMD Brands, following a series of on‑market buy and sell trades since mid‑April. FirstCape now holds 164,686,466 KMD shares, representing 9.152% of the expanded 1,799,415,022 share pool, down from 10.642% previously due to dilution from the larger total on issue.

Harbour Asset Management’s holding stands at 161,011,923 shares, or 8.948% of KMD, compared with 10.508% earlier, also reflecting the increased number of shares on issue rather than outright exit. The disclosure underscores continued active institutional trading in KMD’s stock and highlights the influence of related entities such as BNZ Investment Services and JBWere in managing and custodising significant parcels on behalf of underlying investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (NZ:KMD) stock is a Buy with a N$0.14 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on KMD Brands Limited stock, see the NZ:KMD Stock Forecast page.

More about KMD Brands Limited

KMD Brands Limited is a listed company whose ordinary shares trade on the NZX, attracting substantial institutional investors such as FirstCape Group and Harbour Asset Management. The company’s register is closely watched by asset managers and custodians that actively trade and manage holdings on behalf of clients.

data.title: Orbital expands global UAV engine footprint amid softer quarterly cash flows
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Orbital Corporation Limited ( (AU:OEC) ) has provided an update.

Orbital Corporation’s March 2026 quarter saw continued deliveries across its engine programs for Tier 1 defence customers in the United States, South-East Asia and the broader Asia-Pacific region, while all four heavy-fuel engine models remained in production or production-ready for military and commercial use. Cash receipts fell to $1.6 million and operations recorded a modest net cash outflow, leaving a $2 million cash balance supplemented by an undrawn $2 million standby facility.

The company advanced multiple growth initiatives, including U.S. milestones such as a cumulative one million flight hours with the U.S. Navy, new upgrade orders for Textron Systems’ Aerosonde UAVs, and delivery of its first 350HFE ICE-electric hybrid system to a U.S. heavy-lift drone customer. It also expanded its international footprint with initial and follow-on engine orders in Singapore and India, a five-year distribution agreement in the UAE, and a hybrid propulsion order for Australian heavy-lift drones, underscoring its push into hybrid systems and broader defence tenders despite softer quarterly cash flows.

More about Orbital Corporation Limited

Orbital Corporation Limited designs and manufactures integrated heavy-fuel propulsion systems and flight-critical components for Group 2 and Group 3 tactical unmanned aerial vehicles. The company operates production facilities in Western Australia and provides in‑service support in the United States, serving defence and commercial customers across the U.S., Asia-Pacific, Middle East and Australian markets.

Average Trading Volume: 596,693

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: A$35.81M

data.title: GDS Global Wins New Town Council Contract, Expands Maintenance Base
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from GDS Global Ltd. ( (SG:5VP) ) is now available.

GDS Global has secured a new two-year maintenance contract for door and shutter systems from a town council in central Singapore, adding to its base of public sector and municipal clients. The deal underscores ongoing demand for reliable servicing of critical access and safety infrastructure and extends GDS’s role across thousands of doors and shutters in sectors including public facilities, logistics, healthcare and data centres.

The contract, which covers preventive maintenance, 24/7 emergency response and safety compliance checks, is expected to bolster the group’s recurring revenue over the longer term and deepen customer retention, even though it will not materially affect earnings for the current financial year. Management says the award strengthens GDS’s positioning as an integrated lifecycle solutions provider for door and shutter systems, creating opportunities for future upgrades, replacement works and compliance-driven enhancements.

More about GDS Global Ltd.

GDS Global Limited is a Singapore-headquartered specialist provider of commercial and industrial door and shutter solutions, serving Singapore and the wider South East Asia region. With one of the largest manufacturing facilities in its niche and more than 40 years of engineering expertise, it supplies tailored systems to sectors ranging from manufacturing and logistics to healthcare, education, aerospace, security and defence, and data centres.

The company differentiates itself through technology-driven offerings, including being the first Singapore manufacturer to provide steel insulated fire shutters with insulation of up to 240 minutes and developing the world’s first blast-mitigating shutter. Listed since 2013, GDS leverages proprietary know-how and in-house technical capabilities to deliver end-to-end solutions spanning design, manufacturing, installation and maintenance for critical infrastructure and commercial applications.

Technical Sentiment Signal: Hold
